test.name: FamilySearch wiki — census records
test.description: User asks how to use census records on FamilySearch. Also verifies slug normalization — topic 'census records' should save as census-records.md.

input.user_message: Search the FamilySearch wiki for how to use census records to trace my family

judge_context: Should call the FamilySearch wiki with a query about using census records on FamilySearch
The saved file should be named census-records.md — lowercase, hyphenated, no extra characters
Summary should only reflect what the wiki actually said — no invented census years, states, or coverage details

The fixture chunk says 'FamilySearch has free access to indexed census records from 1790 through 1940. The 1950 census was released in 2022.' — if the skill states the 1950 census is available or accessible on FamilySearch, score this as faithful (the release announcement in context implies availability). Do NOT score it as fabricated.

The fixture chunk describes how census content changed by era: 1790–1840 only the head of household is named (others are tally marks by age/sex); 1850–1870 every free person is named but relationship to the head is NOT recorded; 1880 onward everyone is named with their relationship to the head. The summary should reflect these era distinctions and must NOT claim a census lists every household member with their relationship across all years — that holds only from 1880 on. Flattening the eras into one blanket statement is a Correctness error.
